title: Podman fails to pull images from docker.io if they aren't cached at mirror.gcr.io
body:
### Issue Description

Podman gives an error when pulling an image from docker.io that doesn't exist on the mirror.gcr.io mirror.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman pull docker.io/fallenbagel/jellyseerr`

### Describe the results you received

`Error: initializing source docker://fallenbagel/jellyseerr:latest: reading manifest latest in mirror.gcr.io/fallenbagel/jellyseerr: manifest unknown: Failed to fetch "latest" from request "/v2/fallenbagel/jellyseerr/manifests/latest".`

### Describe the results you expected

I expect the image to start pulling either from the mirror or fallback to docker.io instead of receiving an error that isn't obvious for an idiot like to me resolve.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

The issue is easily resolved by changing `/etc/containers/registries.conf` lines from

```
prefix="docker.io"
location="mirror.gcr.io"
```
to

```
prefix="docker.io"
location="docker.io"
```

I assume it's because the image isn't popular enough to be cached or isn't cached for another reason at the mirror.  But I would expect podman to fallback to docker.io in that case.  Unless it's a problem with the mirror not grabbing and caching the image?  Which does seem to be the expected behaviour if I am reading the link in the `/etc/containers/registries.conf` file right (https://cloud.google.com/container-registry/docs/pulling-cached-images).

comments.body:
@shaunmayo, thank you for reaching out.

I assume that you want to use `mirror.gcr.io` as a _mirror_.  You configured it as follows
```TOML
prefix="docker.io"
location="mirror.gcr.io"
```
This is not setting it as a mirror but entirely replacing `docker.io` with `mirror.gcr.io`.    Instead, configure it as follow:
```TOML
location="docker.io"
[[registry.mirror]]
location = "mirror.gcr.io"
```

Please refer to the [registries.conf man page ](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-registries.conf.5.md)for details.

Thanks for the reply. 

I didn't make any changes to registries.conf.  Rather the default configuration didn't work and wanted to report this.

My bad for calling it a mirror in this context, I get it now haha.

> I didn't make any changes to registries.conf. Rather the default configuration didn't work and wanted to report this.

@shaunmayo, can you elaborate on that?  The default registries.conf on Fedora does not set any `[registry]`.  Somebody must have added the below lines to the file.

```TOML
prefix="docker.io"
location="mirror.gcr.io"
```
I am using Gentoo but i assumed it was the same defaults as https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/registries.conf

> https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/registries.conf

This registries.conf is only used in the upstream CI.   All requests to docker.io go to `mirror.gcr.io` instead.  We do this to avoid hitting rate limits on Docker Hub.

That makes sense.  It was just a little frustrating working out why it wasn't able to pull the image on Gentoo without making any changes.  

Thank you.
I understand the frustration.  Please report the issue to the Gentoo maintainers for them to take a look.  There is nothing we can do upstream, unfortunately.

FYI for anyone coming across this issue on Gentoo.  I have given additional information to the already existing issue here https://bugs.gentoo.org/849863

It seems the upstream `registries.conf` example is unsuitable for distribution as it is used for testing only in order to prevent hitting the docker hub rate limits.
